bull_thesis: The thesis is the SoP discount unlock, not the franchise. On 09/24/2025 KBR's board UNANIMOUSLY approved a tax-free spin-off of Mission Technology Solutions (MTS) into a standalone public company, creating two pure-plays: New KBR (Sustainable Technology Solutions / STS) and SpinCo (MTS, govt national-security & space). Today the blended conglomerate trades at ~$32.62 (mkt cap ~$4.14B, fwd P/E ~7.95) — its 52-week LOW ($29.94) and ~39% off its 52-week high ($53.50). The two segments, valued at conservative peer multiples, sum to far more than the whole: MTS $571M adj EBITDA (10% margin) at govt-services peer ~11-13x (CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen) = ~$6.3-7.4B EV; STS $479M adj EBITDA (22% margin, ~85 proprietary process-tech licenses) at process-tech/clean-energy ~10-12x = ~$4.8-5.8B EV; combined ~$11.1-13.2B EV less ~$1.6-1.8B net debt = ~$9.3-11.4B equity, ~$73-90/share at peer medians on ~127M shares. Independent SoP work corroborates a ~$59 base case (~80% upside) with $71-88 aggressive. The catalyst is the most reliable kind: tax-free, board-approved, no acquirer financing or antitrust contingency, no third-party counterparty. Post-spin index/passive flows and pure-play re-rating force the multiple toward peer medians. A director bought 3,000 shares in May 2026; post-spin CEO (Bradie) and CFO (Evans, eff. Jan 5 2026) are named. You are buying the trough multiple with the unlock optionality nearly free.
bear_thesis: The unlock could disappoint or slip. Timing is a quarter-band ('mid-to-late 2026'), NOT a pinned date, and the Form 10 has not yet been declared effective — slippage on audited carve-out financials, the debt-allocation split between New KBR and SpinCo, or a soft equity-issuance window could push the separation and bleed the discount. Near-term MTS revenue is pressured by government-related project delays — the reason the street cut targets from $78 to $65 — which could blunt SpinCo's defense-peer multiple at launch. If net debt is loaded onto SpinCo, it may trade poorly out of the gate. STS is energy-transition / ammonia capex-cycle exposed; a soft clean-energy backdrop compresses its multiple below the 11-12x assumed. Dis-synergies and stranded corporate cost erode the headline SoP. In the failure case the parts simply DON'T re-rate and the stock stays a low-multiple conglomerate at ~$30-33, near where it trades now — limited further downside, but the upside never crystallizes.
sop_bull: Event-win value = peer-median pure-play re-rating. MTS $571M adj EBITDA x ~12x = ~$6.85B EV; STS $479M adj EBITDA x ~11.5x = ~$5.5B EV; combined ~$12.35B EV less ~$1.7B net debt = ~$10.65B equity / ~127M sh = ~$84/share gross. Haircut ~30-37% for execution dis-synergies, stranded cost, holdco/spin discount and timing => a disciplined event-resolved TARGET of ~$53/share (also below independent ~$59 base and street $65). ~60%+ upside from spot.
sop_bear: Verified downside FLOOR = standalone going-concern value if the spin slips or the parts refuse to re-rate. KBR remains profitable (TTM rev $7.69B, net income ~$400M, EPS $3.13, $0.66 div) and trades at ~7.9x fwd earnings near its 52-wk low ($29.94). The blended business at the trough multiple already prices in failure to unlock SoP. Floor ~$33.20 ~= current trading; harder support at the $29.94 low / ~7-8x forward earnings. No deal-break cash cliff (no acquirer) and no going-concern risk bound the loss.
sop_breakdown: Base-case = probability-weighted(target $53.00, floor $33.20). Assigning ~70% to a successful in-window spin + at-least-partial peer re-rate and ~30% to slip/no-re-rate => ~0.70*53.00 + 0.30*33.20 = ~$47.1 probability-weighted; the Catalyst-Watch base of $53.00 is the event-RESOLVED target (conservative vs $59 independent base and $73-90 gross peer-median SoP). Parts: MTS (govt services, $571M EBITDA, def-peer multiple) ~ $48-55/sh contribution; STS (process-tech licensing, $479M EBITDA, 22% margin) ~ $38-43/sh contribution; less net debt ~$13-14/sh; gross ~$73-90/sh before the execution/holdco haircut that yields the disciplined $53 target.
risk_reward: Upside-to-target $53.00 vs spot $32.62 = +$20.38 (+62%); downside-to-floor $33.20 = -$0.58 essentially flat to current trading (hard support $29.94 = -8%). R:R on target-vs-floor is extreme (the floor is at spot). Even on the harder $29.94 support, ~+62% up vs ~-8% down = ~7.5:1 asymmetry. Catalyst-Watch SoP R:R 6.54 corroborated.
catalyst_summary: Board-approved tax-free spin-off of Mission Technology Solutions into a standalone public co, targeted mid-to-late 2026 (Form 10 effectiveness pending); stock at 52-wk-low ~8x fwd earnings vs peer-median SoP ~$73-90 and a conservative $53 target = ~62% up vs a floor at spot.
dated_milestone: Spin-off completion targeted mid-to-late 2026 (board-approved 09/24/2025, tax-free); gating step = Form 10 declared effective (not yet effective as of 06/22/2026); precise record/effective date not yet declared.
forcing_function: Effectiveness of the SEC Form 10 registration for SpinCo (Mission Technology Solutions) and the subsequent distribution/record date inside the mid-to-late-2026 window — the moment the two pure-plays begin trading separately and passive/index + defense-peer and process-tech-peer re-rating flows force the conglomerate discount to close.
consensus_delta: The street still values KBR as a blended conglomerate at a trough ~7.9x forward multiple and trimmed targets to $65 on near-term govt project delays — anchoring on the cyclical revenue soft patch while UNDER-WEIGHTING the structural SoP unlock the board has already approved. The wrong assumption is that the blended multiple is the right lens: MTS deserves the defense-services peer multiple (CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen ~11-13x) and STS the higher process-tech/licensing multiple (~10-12x, 22% margins), and only the act of separation lets each be priced correctly. Consensus is pricing the overhang (spin uncertainty + project delays) but not the de-conglomeratization re-rate; independent SoP at $59-90 vs an $32.62 tape is the gap.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 mo) risks are timing and optics, not solvency. (1) Form 10 not yet effective + a quarter-band ('mid-to-late 2026') window => slippage risk that bleeds the arb and frustrates event-driven holders. (2) Continued government-related project delays soften MTS revenue and could depress SpinCo's launch multiple. (3) Undisclosed debt-allocation between New KBR and SpinCo — an over-levered SpinCo trades poorly out of the gate. (4) A soft energy-transition/ammonia capex cycle compresses the STS multiple. None of these is a cash-burn hump or maturity wall — KBR is FCF-positive, profitable, and pays a dividend; the 'valley' is re-rate-deferral, not capital destruction.
positioning_washout: The shareholder base is largely value/GARP and dividend holders anchored to a low-multiple industrial conglomerate; the stock is already at its 52-week low, suggesting tax-loss and disappointment selling has substantially flushed the weak hands (mkt cap down ~39% from the high). The spin itself is the mechanical event: index/passive funds must hold or trade SpinCo on its inclusion, and dedicated defense-services and process-tech funds become natural new owners of the separated pure-plays — a constructive ownership transfer rather than a forced liquidation. A May-2026 insider purchase signals the washout is near its floor.
moderator_conclusion: This is a textbook defined-risk, asymmetric special situation and the funnel should not reflexively cap it like a priced compounder. The catalyst is real, board-approved, tax-free, and inside an explicit mid-to-late-2026 window, with no acquirer-financing or antitrust contingency and no third-party counterparty — the most reliable catalyst archetype. The two segments sum to ~$73-90/share at peer medians (independent base ~$59), the disciplined event-resolved target is $53, and the stock trades at $32.62 — its 52-week low and ~8x forward earnings — which IS the downside floor on a still-profitable, dividend-paying going concern. Upside ~+62% to target vs a floor essentially at spot (~7.5:1 on the harder $29.94 support). The one debit keeping conviction at 4 rather than 5 is that the catalyst, while board-binding and in-window, lacks a PINNED record/effective date (Form 10 not yet effective) — so it is PENDING_HARD, not a pinned-date imminent fire. value_conviction is 5: the margin of safety to the VERIFIED downside floor is the strongest part of the setup — you are buying at the trough multiple with the SoP unlock as near-free optionality, and the floor is a going concern, not a recovery hope. Verdict A (Aggressive Entry), conviction 4, value_conviction 5.
interrogator_dossier:
# KBR Inc (KBR) - Catalyst Interrogation Dossier

**Frame:** Event-driven special situation. The thesis is NOT KBR's franchise quality or compounding - it is the SPREAD between the live ~$32.62 price and the event-resolved sum-of-the-parts value unlocked by the tax-free spin-off of Mission Technology Solutions (MTS) into a standalone public company. The catalyst, not the moat, carries the thesis.

## 1. THE CATALYST - IS IT REAL, DATED, AND BINDING?
Real: YES. On September 24, 2025, KBR's Board UNANIMOUSLY approved the strategic intent to spin off its Mission Technology Solutions segment into an independent, publicly traded company. Confirmed in the FY2025 10-K, an 8-K (Exhibit 99, 09/24/2025), and a dedicated spin-off investor presentation. Stuart Bradie continues as Chair/CEO of New KBR (STS); Shad Evans named CFO eff. Jan 5, 2026.
Structure: Tax-free to KBR and shareholders for U.S. federal income tax (subject to IRS opinion). Two pure-plays: New KBR (STS) = ~85 proprietary process technologies, energy transition/ammonia/chemicals/refining/circular economy, capital-light licensing, high margin; SpinCo (MTS) = government services, national security & space, long-duration diversified contracts, predictable cash flow, strong backlog.
Dated? PARTIALLY. Completion guided to 'mid-to-late 2026' - a stated, management-committed window - but no record/effective date published yet and the Form 10 has NOT been declared effective (the gating regulatory step). Board-approved + explicit calendar window, but precise effective date undeclared. Just short of a fully PINNED hard date.
Binding? A board-approved tax-free spin is among the most reliable catalysts: no acquirer financing contingency, no antitrust second-request, no third-party counterparty. Primary risks are timing slippage (Form 10 review, carve-out financials, debt allocation) and a soft equity window - NOT M&A deal-break risk.

## 2. SPONSOR / MANAGEMENT CREDIBILITY
KBR is profitable and cash-generative (TTM rev $7.69B, net income ~$400M, EPS $3.13, $0.66 div). Not a going-concern or financing-contingent situation. Post-spin leadership and CFO named; timeline reaffirmed through the FY2026 proxy cycle. A director BOUGHT 3,000 shares in May 2026 - an insider buying the discount. Credibility on executing the spin is HIGH; the only soft spot is timing precision (a quarter-band, not a date).

## 3. VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR
Not a deal-break cash value (no acquirer); the standalone going-concern value if the spin slips or the parts refuse to re-rate. KBR generates ~$7.7B revenue, ~$1.05B combined segment adj EBITDA (MTS $571M @10% + STS $479M @22%), positive net income, pays a dividend. At ~$32.62 (mkt cap ~$4.14B, fwd P/E ~7.95) the stock is near its 52-week LOW ($29.94), ~39% off the high ($53.50). Floor ~$33.20 ~= current trading; harder support at $29.94 / ~7-8x forward earnings - a multiple that already prices in FAILURE to unlock SoP. The asymmetry: buying the trough multiple with the unlock nearly free.

## 4. SUM-OF-THE-PARTS (event-resolved target)
MTS ($571M adj EBITDA): govt-services peers (CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen) ~11-13x => ~$6.3-7.4B EV. STS ($479M adj EBITDA, 22% margin, licensing): process-tech/clean-energy peers ~10-12x => ~$4.8-5.8B EV. Combined EV ~$11.1-13.2B; less ~$1.6-1.8B net debt => equity ~$9.3-11.4B; / ~127M sh => gross SoP ~$73-90/share at peer medians. Independent analyst SoP: ~$59 base (~80% up), $71-88 aggressive; sell-side cut $78->$65 on near-term softness/overhang. Catalyst-Watch target $53.00 is a deliberately discounted figure (below $59 base, $65 street, $73-90 gross) - haircutting execution dis-synergies, stranded cost, holdco/spin discount, timing.

## 5. RED / GREEN FLAGS
RED: timing band not a date (Form 10 un-effective) = slippage risk; govt-spend/project-delay headwind on MTS (cause of target cuts); undisclosed debt-allocation between the two entities; STS energy-transition capex exposure. GREEN: board-approved tax-free no-counterparty/financing-risk structure; insider buy May 2026; named post-spin CEO/CFO slate; trading at 52-wk low / ~8x fwd earnings.

## 6. CATALYST VERDICT
Board-approved, tax-free, dual-pure-play spin in an explicit mid-to-late-2026 window; stock at its trough multiple; peer-median SoP ~2x spot and a conservative $53 target ~60% above spot vs a floor essentially at today's price on a profitable, dividend-paying going concern. Genuine asymmetric defined-risk special situation. The only thing short of a pinned conviction-5 is the absence of a declared record/effective date (Form 10 not yet effective). Status: PENDING_HARD (in-window, board-approved; Form 10 effectiveness pending).

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: The catalyst date in the dossier is STALE/WRONG. The file asserts a board-approved spin 'targeted mid-to-late 2026' and treats the catalyst as in-window. On the Q1 FY2026 earnings call (05/05/2026) CEO Stuart Bradie stated verbatim: 'we are working toward an effective spin date of January 4, 2027' — a ~6-month SLIP out of the assumed 2026 window, to the first business day of fiscal 2027. Worse, the Form 10 is STILL CONFIDENTIAL: management 'anticipate[s] a public filing in September' 2026, so the gating regulatory step (public Form 10 + effectiveness) has not even begun publicly as of 06/22/2026. The spread has NOT closed (stock still $32.62, at/near its 52-wk low $29.94) and the going-concern floor IS real (Q1 net income $102M, EPS $0.80, $0.66 div, FCF+), so the asymmetry survives — but the 'PENDING_HARD / in-window' framing and the 'mid-to-late 2026' milestone are false. This is a 2027 event with the first public milestone ~3 months out, NOT an imminent in-window fire; carry-cost and further-slip risk are higher than the file represents.

catalyst.dated_milestone: Spin-off completion targeted mid-to-late 2026 (board-approved 09/24/2025, tax-free); gating step = Form 10 declared effective (not yet effective as of 06/22/2026); precise record/effective date not yet declared.
catalyst.binding_reason: Board-approved tax-free MTS spin with a strong value_conviction (5) — buying the 52-wk-low ~8x trough multiple with a peer-median SoP ~$73-90 (disciplined $53 target) as near-free optionality, floor essentially at spot. BUT the Skeptic capped at 3 (~60) on a STALE DATE: the spin slipped to a targeted 2027-01-04 effective date (CEO, 05/05/26) and the Form 10 is STILL CONFIDENTIAL (public filing only ~Sep-2026) — the gating regulatory step has not begun publicly, so the 'in-window PENDING_HARD' framing is false. Pushed just below the seat line: no public milestone for ~3 months, real further-slip and carry-cost risk, and live MTS revenue softening.

bull_thesis: KBR is splitting in two, and the parts are worth meaningfully more than the bundled whole. On January 4, 2027 the company will spin off its Mission Technology Solutions arm (government services - national security and space, roughly $5.8 billion of revenue) as a separate public company, leaving 'New KBR' with the high-margin Sustainable Technology Solutions business (process-technology licensing for ammonia, chemicals, refining and clean energy, about $2.2 billion of revenue at a ~22% margin). Today the market values the combined company at a single blended multiple of about 10x EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization) - a conglomerate discount that flatters neither half. Once they trade separately, the technology licensor can be valued against richer process-tech and clean-energy peers (~11-14x) and the government-services half against defense-services comparables CACI, Leidos and Booz Allen (~10-12x). The catalyst is hard and dated: the board approved it unanimously in September 2025, the standalone leadership team (CEO-designate Michael LaRouche and CFO-designate Nicholas Veasey) was named on June 25, 2026, and management has reaffirmed the January 2027 completion. Naming the standalone executives is the concrete step companies take only when a spin is genuinely proceeding. With the stock at roughly $33 - near its 52-week low of $29.94 and well below both the Street's ~$46 average target and a full parts value in the high-$70s - the asymmetry is wide and the trigger is on the calendar.
bear_thesis: The case fails mainly through delay, not collapse. The timeline has already slipped once - original guidance was 'mid-to-late 2026' and the effective date has moved to January 4, 2027 - and a further slip would push the re-rating out and let the discount linger. The spun-off government-services company is the lower-margin half (about 10%), will carry standalone overhead and likely debt as a brand-new public entity, and may not re-rate cleanly to an 11x peer multiple right away; a newly public, lower-margin contractor often trades at a discount until it proves its standalone numbers. The full standalone financial carve-outs in the Form 10 registration statement are the swing factor for sentiment into the event, and weak disclosures could compress the SpinCo multiple. Energy-transition process-tech multiples for the New KBR half are also contested and could derate if clean-energy sentiment sours. In short, even though the businesses keep operating regardless (there is no deal to break), the spread can stay open longer than expected and the rich end of the parts math may not be realized on day one.
consensus_delta: Wall Street still values KBR as one blended company at roughly 10x EBITDA and ~10-11x earnings - the average analyst target of ~$46 implies the market is only part-way to pricing the separation, and the live price near $33 (a 52-week low) prices essentially none of it. The disagreement is structural, not a story: the bundle's single multiple flatters neither a high-margin (~22%) technology licensor nor a stable government-services contractor. Verified trailing segment EBITDA (STS ~$479M, MTS ~$571M) valued against the correct peer sets (process-tech/clean-energy at 11-14x for New KBR; CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen at 10-12x for SpinCo) sums to ~$12B of enterprise value, or the high-$70s per share at a full re-rate - well above where the Street sits. The market is underpricing both the certainty of the dated January 2027 trigger and the size of the discount it unlocks.
forcing_function: The January 4, 2027 separation date - the hard, scheduled event on which KBR splits into two independently traded companies, forcing each half to be valued against its own peer group and unlocking the conglomerate discount. Interim re-rate triggers: the Form 10 registration filing with standalone carve-out financials and the when-issued trading window ahead of the spin.
valley_of_death: Low. There is no cash-burn hump, no debt-maturity wall forcing the timeline, and no financing contingency - both businesses are profitable (combined ~$1B+ EBITDA) and the company pays a ~2% dividend throughout. The near-term risks are sentiment, not solvency: (1) a further slip in the January 2027 date (the timeline already moved one quarter from the original mid-2026 guidance), which would delay the re-rate rather than break the thesis; and (2) weak standalone carve-out financials in the Form 10 that could compress the SpinCo multiple before the spin. Neither decimates free cash flow; the floor (~$33, the current trough) holds because the operating businesses keep running regardless.
positioning_washout: Limited and largely behind us. KBR has spent most of 2026 in a $30-34 band near its 52-week low, so weak-handed and momentum holders have already been shaken out - the stock is down sharply from its $52 high. The spin itself creates mechanical flow rather than forced selling: when the separation completes, index funds and government-services-focused mandates that want SpinCo (and energy-transition mandates that want New KBR) will need to establish positions, and some holders of the combined name may rotate - that index/mandate reshuffle around January 2027 is a source of buying support, not a liquidation overhang. The dossier's index-flow tag points to this re-rating mechanic.
moderator_conclusion: This is the funnel working as intended - a defined-risk, dated special situation that earns an aggressive-entry call rather than a reflexive compounder cap. The catalyst is a hard, scheduled corporate event: KBR splits into two independent public companies on January 4, 2027, the board approved it unanimously, the standalone leadership team was named on June 25, 2026, and management has reaffirmed the timeline. The remaining gates (IRS tax ruling, SEC Form 10 effectiveness, board sign-off) are customary, not regulatory or financing hurdles - there is no deal that can be voted down, so the risk is slippage, not failure. The live price near $33 sits at a 52-week trough, well below both the Street's ~$46 target and a parts value (~$12B enterprise value, high-$70s per share at a full re-rate) that the conglomerate discount is suppressing. Critically, the floor is real and close: with no deal to break, the businesses keep generating $1B+ of EBITDA, so ~$33 is the standalone-bundle trough, giving roughly $20 of upside to the $53 target against essentially no downside to the floor. I dock conviction from the maximum only because the timeline has already slipped one quarter and the lower-margin SpinCo half may not re-rate cleanly on day one - delay risk, not break risk. Buy now: a live, dated catalyst inside the window, price well below the event-resolved value, with a verified floor. Value conviction matches at 4 - the margin of safety to the verified floor is wide because you are paying the trough price for two profitable franchises.
sop_breakdown: Base case ~$49 per share, probability-weighted between the verified target zone (~$53 dossier / ~$46-47 Street) at ~75% odds the spin completes on schedule and re-rates partially, and the ~$33 standalone-bundle floor at ~25% odds of a meaningful slip / muted re-rate. Parts: New KBR / Sustainable Technology Solutions ~$479M EBITDA x ~12x = ~$5.7B EV; SpinCo / Mission Technology Solutions ~$571M EBITDA x ~11x = ~$6.3B EV; combined ~$12B EV less ~$1.8B net debt / ~127M shares. Full re-rate optionality to the high-$70s if both halves reach peer medians.
risk_reward: Upside to the ~$53 target is about +59% from ~$33.37; downside to the ~$33.2 floor is roughly flat (about -0.5%). Asymmetry is strongly positive - roughly $20 of upside to target against essentially no downside to the verified trough floor (defined risk). To the base-case ~$49 fair value, ~+47% with the floor at the current price.
interrogator_dossier:
# KBR, Inc. (KBR) — Event-Driven Special Situation Dossier

**Setup:** Sum-of-the-parts spin-off. KBR is splitting itself in two. The thesis is the gap between today's blended price (~$33) and what the two halves are worth once they trade separately and each gets valued against its own peer group.

## 1. THE EVENT — what is being separated

On September 24, 2025, KBR's board unanimously approved a plan to spin off its Mission Technology Solutions business (the government-services arm — national security and space, roughly $5.8 billion in annual revenue and ~20,000 employees) into a separate, independent, publicly traded company. What remains — "New KBR" — keeps the Sustainable Technology Solutions business, a high-margin licensing and process-technology franchise (ammonia, chemicals, refining, clean-energy / circular-economy process tech, with 85-plus licensed processes).

The logic is a classic conglomerate-discount unlock: a slow-growth-but-stable government services contractor and a high-margin, IP-driven energy-transition technology licensor do not belong in the same wrapper. Each appeals to a different buyer, and the market currently values the bundle at a single blended multiple (~10x EBITDA, ~10-11x earnings) that flatters neither. Split apart, the defense-services half can be valued against CACI, Leidos and Booz Allen, and the technology half against process-tech and clean-energy peers that trade richer.

## 2. CATALYST STATUS — is it real, dated, and binding?

This is the strongest part of the case. The catalyst is no longer a vague "strategic intent." As of the most recent disclosures it is a firm, scheduled corporate event with a named completion date and a named management team:

- **Completion date: January 4, 2027.** The separation is scheduled to take effect on that date — a hard, dated event roughly six months out.
- **June 25, 2026:** KBR named the leadership of the spun-off company — Michael LaRouche (from Serco North America, ex-SAIC/Raytheon/Lockheed) as CEO-designate and Nicholas Veasey (ex-MAG Aerospace CFO) as CFO-designate. Naming the standalone executive team is a concrete step that companies take only when a spin is genuinely proceeding, not stalling.
- **July 1, 2026 / September 24, 2026:** Veasey and LaRouche join, respectively, to stand up the independent company ahead of launch.
- **Earnings-call confirmation:** management has reaffirmed the separation is proceeding on schedule.

The remaining gates are the customary, low-drama ones for a tax-free U.S. spin: a favorable IRS private-letter ruling / tax opinion, the effectiveness of the Form 10 registration statement with the SEC, and final board sign-off. None of these is a regulatory antitrust hurdle or a financing contingency — there is no acquirer, no second request, no deal that can be voted down. The risk here is *slippage*, not *failure*.

One timing nuance worth flagging honestly: the original September 2025 guidance said "mid-to-late 2026," and the date has since moved to early January 2027 — a roughly one-quarter slip. That is mild for a spin of this size, and the appointment of the standalone leadership team in June 2026 reads as the company de-risking the timeline rather than letting it drift indefinitely. It is a real, dated, binding event inside a defined window.

**Verdict on the event: a hard, dated catalyst (a firm, scheduled event).** Not an already-fired re-rate, not a non-binding soft promise.

## 3. THE FLOOR — what actually backstops the price if the event disappoints

The downside here is unusually well-defined for a special situation, because there is no deal to break — the two businesses keep operating regardless. The floor is the *standalone-bundle* value, not zero.

KBR has spent most of 2026 trading in a $30-34 band, near its 52-week low of $29.94 (the high was $52.23). At ~$33, the whole company trades at roughly 10x EBITDA and ~10-11x earnings — i.e., the market is already pricing the conglomerate discount, not a premium. Even if the spin slipped a year, the combined business still generates a little over $1 billion of EBITDA and pays a ~2% dividend, so the price is supported by ongoing cash earnings, not by hope. The verified downside floor is roughly the current trough — call it ~$33 — below which you are buying a profitable, cash-generative defense-plus-tech conglomerate at a single-digit-to-low-double-digit multiple. That is a real backstop, not a CVR or a recovery estimate on a broken deal.

## 4. THE SPREAD — upside to the parts

The parts are worth more than the whole. Using verified trailing segment EBITDA:

- **New KBR / Sustainable Technology Solutions:** ~$479M EBITDA on ~$2.2B revenue at a ~22% margin — a high-margin IP licensor. Process-tech / clean-energy peers support ~11-14x. At ~12x that is ~$5.7B of enterprise value.
- **SpinCo / Mission Technology Solutions:** ~$571M EBITDA on ~$5.8B revenue (~10% margin) — government services valued against CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen at ~10-12x. At ~11x that is ~$6.3B of enterprise value.

Combined enterprise value lands around $12 billion. Subtract roughly $1.7-1.9 billion of net debt and divide by ~127 million shares, and the parts imply something in the high-$70s to mid-$80s per share if both halves re-rate fully to peer medians — clearly above the dossier's published $53 target and Wall Street's ~$46-47 average target. The reason targets sit below the full re-rate is sensible: spins rarely close the entire gap on day one, SpinCo carries debt and a dis-synergy cost-base as a new standalone, and the energy-transition multiple for STS is contested. The honest reading is that the verified target zone is ~$47-53 (Street to dossier), with genuine optionality to the $70s if the re-rate runs.

## 5. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

- Green: dated completion (Jan 4, 2027), standalone team named, board-approved, tax-free, no antitrust or financing gate, profitable on both sides, price already at the trough.
- Green: the conglomerate discount is demonstrable — blended ~10x vs. parts at 11-14x — so the mispricing is structural, not a story.
- Amber: timeline has already slipped one quarter (mid-2026 → Jan 2027); a further slip is the main risk and would push out the re-rate, not destroy it.
- Amber: SpinCo (MTS) is the lower-margin half (~10%) and will carry standalone costs and likely debt; its 11x peer multiple assumes it re-rates cleanly, which a newly-public, lower-margin government contractor may not do immediately.
- Amber: the full Form 10 financial carve-outs are the swing factor for sentiment into the event; weak standalone disclosures could compress the SpinCo multiple.

## 6. MOAT / SECULAR (largely N/A for an event)

This is a defined-risk special situation, not a compounder bet, so franchise moat is secondary. For completeness: New KBR's technology-licensing IP is a narrow but real moat (switching costs and installed-base licensing), and the government-services half is a scale/relationship/clearance moat that is stable rather than eroding. No terminal secular threat — if anything, both halves (energy-transition process tech, national-security/space services) sit in structurally supported end-markets. The cheapness is a structure-and-bundling artifact, not a melting base.

**Bottom line for the director:** A hard, dated, board-approved tax-free spin (effective January 4, 2027) with standalone leadership already named, a live price (~$33) sitting at its 52-week trough and well below both Street targets (~$46) and a parts value in the high-$70s, with a real floor at the current standalone-bundle price. The defined-risk asymmetry is strong; the only meaningful risk is a further timeline slip, which delays rather than kills the thesis.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
